summary: User policy for common control and window animations. Good for reduced motion, accessibility, and some battery-sensitive scenarios, but it removes animated UI feedback for the current user.

setting.name: Common Control and Window Animations
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can animate common controls and some window transitions. This policy decides whether those animations are turned off for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable these animations to reduce motion, improve usability for some visual disabilities, or make Windows feel less busy.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Disabling animations can make Windows calmer and in some cases help usability or battery life, but it also removes animated visual feedback.
setting.professional_notes: Explorer.admx maps the user policy to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\TurnOffSPIAnimations with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0. The local ADML help text explicitly mentions accessibility, performance, and battery-life benefits in some scenarios.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows is not forcing this animation policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Animations allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and common control and window animations remain on.

allowed_values.label: Animations turned off
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is enabled and common control and window animations are turned off for the current user.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes TurnOffSPIAnimations = 1 for the current user, which matches the documented enabled policy state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows Vista and newer user profiles

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The local policy definition explains the effects of enabling the setting, while the user-policy baseline stays unset by default.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Users who want the normal animated desktop feel
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the standard Windows animation behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a calmer or more accessibility-friendly interface.

recommended_profiles.label: Reduced motion
avoid_for: Users who prefer more visual feedback

states.rationale: Turn off common control and window animations for the current user.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want less motion or potentially better usability for some visual needs.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You like animated UI transitions and feedback.
tradeoffs: Windows feels calmer and less animated.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Explorer.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user policy to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\TurnOffSPIAnimations with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Explorer.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says disabling animations can improve usability for some visual disabilities as well as performance and battery life in some scenarios.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes TurnOffSPIAnimations = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft Explorer policy files explicitly define the exact user-policy surface, allowed values, and behavior for TurnOffSPIAnimations. The app writes the documented enabled state for turning off common control and window animations.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="TurnOffSPIAnimations" class="User" displayName="$(string.TurnOffSPIAnimations)" explainText="$(string.TurnOffSPIAnimations_Help)" key="Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer" valueName="TurnOffSPIAnimations">; Explorer.adml: This policy is similar to settings directly available to computer users. Disabling animations can improve usability for users with some visual disabilities as well as improving performance and battery life in some scenarios.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name, and local ADML confirms the reduced-motion, accessibility, and performance/battery tradeoffs. The app writes the documented enabled state under the same user-policy surface.
